commit.message:
Add 24-hour cooldown between failure notifications per workflow (#5)

* Initial plan

* Add 24-hour cooldown between failure notifications

- Add .github/actions/check-cooldown/action.yml: composite action that queries the GitHub API to check whether the email notification step specifically succeeded in any workflow run within the last 24 hours. Checks step conclusion (not job conclusion) to avoid permanently suppressing notifications after the first cooldown cycle.
- Update process_queue.yml and sync_github.yml: add checkout + cooldown check steps to the NotifyOnFailure job, and make the email step conditional on should_notify == 'true'.

commit.message:
Don't include inferred 'who' in the involves list (#63)

The involved list is used by the ?involves=true filter to show PRs
a user has explicitly participated in (authored, reviewed, commented,
or was requested to review).  It was also including the 'who' field,
which is an inferred suggestion of who should act next.  This caused
maintainers who happen to have write access (e.g. from merging a few
of their own PRs) to see every unowned PR in their involves view,
even in repos they don't actively review.

Remove the who -> involvedSet propagation so that involves reflects
only actual participation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(device): add TX ring silence guard to prevent stale-data pops

ExternalBuffer uses unconditional_read=true which bypasses the ring's
readable_pos freshness check. After the first ring revolution (~682 ms)
positions beyond write_pos hold old audio data.  When write_pos stalls
during MXWANI8 silence suppression (block==0), TX reads those stale
positions → pop on every silence→audio transition.

Fix A (silence): when block==0, zero-fill ring positions from write_pos
up to tx_current_pos + LEAD_SAMPLES so TX always reads clean silence
instead of old audio from the previous revolution.

Fix B (gap resumption): when block > 2×LEAD (a gap just ended), snap
write_pos to tx_current_pos + LEAD_SAMPLES before writing.  Without
this, the large catch-up batch advances write_pos far ahead of TX,
causing latency runaway until the ring overflows.

tx_current_pos is read from current_ts_cb (set by flows_tx.rs) and
adjusted by ptp_at_poll to convert absolute PTP timestamp to ring offset.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(im-acp-gateway): Initial Commit (#182)

* docs(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Initial Research Documentation

* docs(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Early Research Verification Documentation

* feat(im-acp-gateway): Introduce WeChat iLink Verification PoC and Documentation

* docs(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Early Research Documentation for Telegram

* feat(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Telegram Bot Verification PoC and Documentation

* feat(im-acp-gateway): Do Telegram Bot Verification PoC for Item 3

* docs(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Requirements Input List

* feat(im-acp-gateway): Introduce Telegram Topic Session Bridge POC

Add the Telegram topic-session bridge proof of concept for the IM ACP gateway.

This introduces one Telegram topic per managed Copilot ACP session, restores
persisted ACP sessions after bridge restart, supports takeover of existing ACP
sessions, renders Telegram-safe MarkdownV2, normalizes Telegram long-dash
command input, exposes concrete ACP permission options, and adds bridge-managed
allow-all mode for topic-level approval automation.

It also keeps session topics focused on explicit Copilot dialogue while retrying
Telegram API rate limits instead of crashing on transient HTTP 429 responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(im-acp-gateway): Address PR Review Comments and CI Failures

- Fix parseJsonResponse to check response.ok before JSON.parse
- Truncate verbose QR error message to exclude large base64 payload
- Add @types/node, @biomejs/biome, @typescript/native-preview as
  devDependencies to all 3 POC packages
- Remove fragile cross-package typeRoots from tsconfig.json
- Simplify package.json scripts to use local pnpm exec
- Fix biome import sorting and formatting
- Fix prettier markdown formatting
- Regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml (includes telegram-topic-session-bridge)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(im-acp-gateway): Fix Markdown Line Length in Research-Telegram.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(im-acp-gateway): Guard POC Tests for Node.js >= 22.6

The POC packages use --experimental-strip-types which requires
Node.js >= 22.6. Update engines field and add a version check in
the test script so tests skip gracefully on older Node.js versions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(im-acp-gateway): Fix Node.js Version Guard Logic in Test Scripts

The previous guard used && which continued on exit(0). Switch to ||
so the guard exits 0 (skip) on Node < 22 and exits 1 (proceed) on
Node >= 22, letting || chain to the actual test command.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(im-acp-gateway): Harden State File Permissions and ACP Client Directory Access

- Write temp files with mode 0o600 directly via writeFile options instead of
  chmod after rename, eliminating the permission race window for state files
  containing bot tokens
- Remove now-unused chmod import from all three state.ts files
- Add workingDirectory and allowedDirectories parameters to bridge's
  CopilotAcpClient.start(), passing --add-dir flags to match the pattern
  already used in telegram-bot-verifier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement all pending TODOs: flatten PE/TE, fix duplicates, API-only warning

1. PE SinglePassMode: When SinglePassMode=true, PE skips step generation
   and produces complete implementation in one AI call (was N calls per step)
2. TE SinglePassMode: When SinglePassMode=true, TE generates unit+integration+UI
   tests in one combined prompt (was 3 separate AI calls)
3. Fix duplicate ready-for-review comments: MarkReadyForReviewAsync now checks
   if PR already has ready-for-review label before posting comment
4. TE API-only warning: Made the fallback warning prominent with emoji + bold
   so reviewers clearly see tests were NOT built or executed
5. UI test app path: PlaywrightRunner auto-resolves project path when configured
   path doesn't exist in workspace (already committed separately)

commit.message:
fix: auto-format with ruff before committing in orchestrator (#68)

The CLI agent doesn't run our pre-commit hooks, so PR #67 failed CI on
ruff formatting.

Fix: `commit_and_push()` now runs `ruff format` and `ruff check --fix`
before `git add -A`. This ensures every agent commit passes formatting
checks regardless of CLI output.

Failures are suppressed — the worktree might contain non-Python files,
and formatting shouldn't block commits.

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Regenerate GLAD bindings without debug wrappers

- Regenerated GLAD 2.0.8 for GL 4.1 core without --debug flag
- Extensions: GL_ARB_texture_filter_anisotropic, GL_ARB_texture_storage
- Dropped GL_KHR_debug (was checked but never actually used)
- Removed r_gl_types.h; merged all GLAD declarations into r_gl.h
- Cleaned up R_GetError_: removed dead KHR_debug check, r_error_count,
  and gladInstallGLDebug() call
- Eliminates ~1.1% CPU overhead from unconditional glGetError() calls
  on every GL function invocation
- Net reduction of ~5,175 lines

- Add: hyprland, hypridle, hyprlock, hyprpaper, uwsm, waybar, wofi,

  xorg-xwayland, gammastep, playerctl, otf-font-awesome
- Remove: xmonad, xmonad-contrib, xorg, xorg-server, xorg-xinit, picom,
  dunst, rofi, feh, redshift, flameshot, xclip, rxvt-unicode,
  urxvt-perls, xterm, code, ttf-sourcecodepro-nerd,
  noto-fonts-cjk, noto-fonts-emoji
- Simplify NVIDIA dialog to nvidia-open (Turing+, recommended),
  nvidia (pre-Turing), or skip (mesa/nouveau default)
- Remove xf86-video-vesa fallback (not applicable on Wayland)
- Remove xmonad pacman hook
- Update unit tests and README accordingly

commit.message:
Wire up SFMLRenderer in main.cpp for end-to-end Renderer abstraction

- Add isOpen(), close(), setMouseCursorVisible() to Renderer base class
- Add pollEvent(sf::Event&) to SFMLRenderer for SFML-specific event polling
- Fix resetView() to use current window size instead of initial default view
- Replace all direct sf::RenderWindow calls in main.cpp with renderer methods:
  window.clear/display -> renderer.clear/display
  window.setView(gameView) -> renderer.setView(cx, cy, viewW, viewH)
  window.setView(sf::View(sf::FloatRect(...))) -> renderer.resetView()
  window.isOpen/close/setMouseCursorVisible -> renderer equivalents
  window.pollEvent -> renderer.pollEvent
- Replace dash ghost sf::RectangleShape rendering with renderer.drawRect()
- Remove sf::View, sf::Color, sf::RectangleShape usage from main.cpp
- SaveSlotScreen still uses renderer.getWindow() for resolution changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: enable parallel heuristic search for criterion=recon (#10)

* feat: enable parallel heuristic search for criterion=recon (criterion 5)

Three changes:
1. reconcile.c get_recon_score(): replace rand() with rand_r(&thread_seed)
   under #ifdef _OPENMP guards so each thread uses its own private PRNG state.
2. reconcile.c get_recon_score(): add temp_top = NULL after each
   duplicate_tree() result is consumed, fixing a double-free that occurred
   when hs_free_thread_state() tried to dismantle the stale pointer left by
   the local temp_top2 shadowing the global threadprivate temp_top.
3. treecompare2.c: remove the forced nthreads=1 fallback for criterion==5.
4. main.c: update nthreads help text to remove "not available for
   criterion=recon".

commit.message:
fix: ASCII description, snapshot cleanup in ENA test

Two bugs found during first live AWS run:

1. RegisterImage description contained em dash (U+2014) — AWS EC2
   rejects non-ASCII chars in description parameter. Replaced with
   ASCII hyphen.

2. EBS snapshot leaked after failures — snapshotId was scoped inside
   the try block so the finally cleanup block couldn't see it.
   Hoisted to outer scope + added deleteSnapshot() call in finally.
   Also adds deleteSnapshot() to src/aws/image.ts.

Manually deleted the leaked snapshot: snap-09e01477c1d0a514c

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: background knowledge base indexing with battery-aware rates

- New battery detection module (src/battery.rs): cross-platform battery status
- New knowledge indexing module (src/knowledge_index.rs): async background indexing
  * Configurable size limits (default 20MB, not surprising)
  * Battery-aware rate adaptation: fast on AC, imperceptibly slow on battery
  * Generates plaintext INDEX.md with category structure and keywords
  * Atomic writes prevent corruption, incremental updates possible
  * Partial coverage with unindexed category fallback guidance
- Config extended (src/config.rs): KnowledgeIndexSettings
  * knowledge_index_enabled: bool
  * knowledge_index_size_limit_gb: f64 (default 0.02 = 20MB)
  * knowledge_index_battery_delay_ms: u64 (default 100ms)
- Integration in main.rs: spawns indexing task on startup
- Steering text updated: documents knowledge index and search strategy
  * Models taught to check INDEX.md first
  * Fallback guidance for unindexed categories
- 50 unit tests passing, zero compilation errors
- 3 new modules added to lib.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use Rekordbox phrase analysis (PSSI) for cue labels when available

- Add read_phrases_anlz(): reads PSSI section from .EXT file via pyrekordbox
- Add _build_beat_ms_lookup(): maps absolute beat index → ms from PQTZ
- Add _PHRASE_LABELS: mood/kind → label table (High/Mid/Low phrase sets)
- Add _trim_phrases(): intelligent MAX_CUES trimming (Intro + Outro always
  kept, then fill by priority: Chorus > Up > Bridge > Down > Verse)
- process_track() now tries PSSI first; falls back to mel cosine analysis
  only when no PSSI data is present (audio file not required for PSSI path)

feat: add 5 production fixes and 4 competitor-matching features

Production Fixes:

- A2: Focus trapping in all modals with trapFocus/releaseFocus utility
- A3: Keyboard navigation (tabindex/role on collapsible headers, keydown handlers)

Competitor Features:

- B2: Email validation before send with modal for issues

Also adds i18n keys for all new UI strings across all 7 languages.

  - coaching_agents.py: prompt construction, round aggregation, LLM agents
  - summary_job.py: async LLM integration, .env loading, OpenAI helpers
- Keep feedback.py as backward-compatibility facade re-exporting all symbols

- Split build_summary_prompt into _build_prompt_header, _build_combo_stats_section,
  _build_kinetic_chain_section, _build_recovery_guard_section
- Split _aggregate_group into _aggregate_velocity, _aggregate_rom,
  _aggregate_recovery, _aggregate_phase_divergence helpers
- Create pose_estimator_protocol.py with PoseEstimator Protocol + MockPoseEstimator
- Add MediaPipePoseEstimator wrapper in pose_estimation.py
- Create coaching_pipeline.py with dependency-injected CoachingPipeline
- Create domain_models.py with PoseSpecialistOutput, UserSession, CoachingContext, FeedbackEvent
- Extract magic numbers to config.py (MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES, VISIBILITY_THRESHOLD, etc.)

- eventbrite.js: remove orphaned duplicate code block (lines 288-340)
- nycparks.js: replace dead RSS scraper with no-op (parks events covered by nycdata tvpp-9vvx)
- songkick.js: remove pages 2-3 (return HTTP 406 from non-browser IPs)
- timeout.js: update to working URLs; add isGarbageTitle filter to OG fallback
- dice.js: dynamic NYC slug discovery; bail early if slug is not NYC (IP redirect goes to DC)

commit.message:
fix: force raster capture on Linux to prevent GPU surface SIGSEGV

GPU-backed SKSurface objects created as intermediate shader capture
layers cause SIGSEGV when finalized on the GC thread, because the GPU
context (GrDirectContext) is not active on the finalizer thread.

On Linux this is reliably triggered by any runtime SkSL shader effect
applied to a panel with children (the more children, the faster the
crash). The raster capture path creates CPU-backed surfaces that are
safe to finalize on any thread, while the runtime shader itself still
executes on the GPU-backed compositor canvas.

The fix adds a static ForceRasterCapture flag that defaults to true on
Linux and can be overridden via EFFECTOR_FORCE_RASTER_CAPTURE env var.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate Map, TransitionManager, and all UI classes to Renderer interface

- Map::render() now takes Renderer& and uses drawRect()/drawRectOutlined()
- TransitionManager::render() takes Renderer&, uses resetView()/drawRect()
  for fade overlay; removed sf::RectangleShape member
- UIStyle.h: all helpers take Renderer&, use float RGBA colors, return
  UIBounds struct; replaced RoundedRectangleShape with drawRoundedRect()
- Deleted RoundedRectangleShape.h (no longer needed)
- PauseMenu: uses Renderer::FontHandle, lazy-loads font, draws via Renderer
- SaveSlotScreen: same pattern; still takes sf::RenderWindow& for resize
- ControlsMenu: same pattern; all drawing via Renderer
- DebugMenu: same pattern; handleEvent() no longer needs window reference
- main.cpp: passes renderer to all render() calls
- Updated architecture.md and moved task to CompletedTasks.md

Return 1x1 transparent pixel instead of 404/403 for expired/missing maps

Fixes #122. When activities expire and get purged, map image URLs
were returning 404/403 errors, making Slack messages look broken.
Now returns a 1x1 transparent PNG pixel in all unavailable-map cases:
- Activity not found (expired/purged)
- Activity hidden/private
- Activity has no polyline
- Activity has no map image data

commit.message:
perf(maglev): extend range analysis to handle Generic* arithmetic ops

Range analysis induction variable detection and forward propagation
now recognize GenericIncrement, GenericDecrement, GenericAdd,
GenericSubtract, and GenericMultiply — not just CheckedSmi* variants.

After global promotion replaces LoadGlobal/StoreGlobal with Phi nodes,
loop counter increments become GenericIncrement(Phi). Previously these
were invisible to range analysis, so they kept overflow checks (JO deopt).
Now they are converted to Int32Increment when the computed range fits i32.

Similarly, accumulator patterns like sum += value are detected through
GenericAdd, enabling Int32Add lowering when total accumulation is bounded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
research: complete Phase 2 doc 05 — statime master code path analysis

- Maps all 4 master.rs functions: send_sync, handle_sync_timestamp,
  send_announce, handle_delay_req
- Identifies PTPv1 incompatibilities: two-step FollowUp, Announce,
  hardcoded version 2.1 in all message builders
- Documents 9-step TX timestamp flow (sync_timer → SendEvent → error queue → FollowUp)
- Key finding: one-step Sync required for PTPv1 (embed timestamp, no FollowUp)
- Lists all 6 files requiring changes with impact levels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
